steps.title: Set up the substitution test
steps.content: A point is a solution to $y \lt -3x + 5$ if substituting its coordinates makes the inequality true. We'll test each answer choice.

steps.title: Backsolving
steps.content:
Backsolving - testing choice B $(0, 6)$: Is $6 \lt -3(0) + 5 = 5$? No, $6 \gt 5$ fails.

Testing choice A $(-3, 0)$: Is $0 \lt -3(-3) + 5 = 9 + 5 = 14$? Yes, $0 \lt 14$ works. That is the correct answer.

steps.title: Check the other choices
steps.content: The other points fail the inequality: $(0,6)$ gives $6$ not less than $5$, $(3,1)$ gives $1$ not less than $-4$, and $(3,-1)$ gives $-1$ not less than $-4$. Only $(-3,0)$ satisfies $y \lt -3x+5$, so A is correct.
